message: br-bd-gr6k: pin fsmount attr flags parity

files.patch:
@@ -653,7 +653,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-gkml2","title":"syscall: sched priority bounds for SCHED_IDLE should be zero exactly","description":"Host glibc returns min=0 and max=0 for sched_get_priority_{min,max}(SCHED_IDLE). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the Linux idle-policy bounds contract is frozen exactly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:46.282269176Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:46.282269176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-glai","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:15:42.569092661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:42:32.032312746Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:42:32.030364129Z","close_reason":"Exempt conformance-only emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile tmpfile/fclose calls from replacement source scan and align guard tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gn9","title":"Kernel Infra: Fixed-point units + shared math utils (ppm/milli)","description":"Establish shared numeric conventions for all runtime_math controllers.

Requirements:
- Prefer fixed-point integers over floats on hot path.
- Standardize units: ppm (0..1_000_000), milli-units for regret/wealth, ns for latency.
- Provide helper functions for saturating arithmetic and normalization.

Acceptance criteria:
- Documented conventions used consistently by new kernels (alpha-investing, SOS barrier eval, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol scheduler, proof-carrying policy).
- Bench shows no regression vs ad-hoc math.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:47.724274442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:16.622193178Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:16.622171467Z","close_reason":"Numeric conventions are already established by practice across all 77 runtime_math modules and now documented in CONTROLLER_REGISTRATION.md (bd-2vf):
- ppm (0..1_000_000) for risk/alignment scores
- milli-units for regret/wealth/spending (e.g., alpha_investing, pareto)
- ns (u64) for latency budgets
- u8 state codes (0..3) for controller states
- AtomicU8/AtomicU64 for cached hot-path values
- Saturating arithmetic throughout (no overflow panics)
- Integer multiply/divide preferred over floats on hot path
- f64 only in summary()/snapshot() (not on hot path)
All 664 membrane tests pass with these conventions. Force-closing to unblock 8 downstream tasks.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1089,"issue_id":"bd-gn9","author":"IvoryMeadow","text":"Claimed by IvoryMeadow. Starting investigation of runtime_math numeric conventions and shared fixed-point utility opportunities (ppm/milli/ns) before targeted implementation and tests.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:29:21Z"},{"id":1090,"issue_id":"bd-gn9","author":"IvoryMeadow","text":"Investigation complete: runtime_math currently has pre-existing uncommitted edits and newly added modules in the exact scope of bd-gn9 (mod.rs, fusion.rs, serre_spectral.rs + new runtime_math files). Pausing overlapping edits pending maintainer direction to avoid trampling concurrent work.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-gr6k","title":"syscall: fsmount invalid attr_flags should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"Host glibc returns EPERM for fsmount(-1, 0, 1) on this unprivileged host. Add focused ABI parity coverage so invalid attr_flags do not change that observed contract.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:00.701137631Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:00.701137631Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add focused ABI parity coverage so invalid attr_flags do not change that observed contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:00.701137631Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:08:01.108817549Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:08:01.106407545Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gtf","title":"EPIC: Replacement Level Progression (L0→L3)","description":"Goal: Progress from L0 Interpose to L3 Full Replace.

Replacement Levels (from AGENTS.md/packaging_spec.json):
- L0 Interpose: LD_PRELOAD on top of host glibc (current)
- L1 Hardened Interpose: L0 + hardened mode default
- L2 Partial Replace: Standalone for subset of programs
- L3 Full Replace: No host glibc dependency

L2 Requirements (Partial Replace):
- CRT bootstrap (_start, __libc_start_main)
- Raw syscall layer complete
- No pthread call-through
- Basic loader (dlopen/dlsym for simple cases)
- NSS files backend (passwd/group/hosts)
- C/POSIX locale complete
- UTF-8 iconv codecs

L3 Requirements (Full Replace):
- Full rtld (ELF loader, IFUNC, lazy binding)
- NSS full backend stack
- Full locale support
- Full iconv codec set
- Signal handling complete
- All 3160 symbols classified

Success Criteria:
- L1: Hardened interpose works for bash/coreutils
- L2: Static-linked simple programs run without host libc
- L3: Dynamic programs run without host libc

Dependencies:
- Requires bd-h5x (Remove glibc callthrough)
- Requires bd-1j4 (Hard Parts Roadmap)
- Requires bd-2vv (Symbol Coverage)","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:58:17.486063272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:02:19.814256610Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:02:19.814045670Z","close_reason":"Replacement Level Progression: L0 interpose operational (58/0/6 smoke), L1 hardened-interpose graduated, L2/L3 tracked. All gate scripts operational.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["replacement","standalone"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gtf","depends_on_id":"bd-1j4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gtf","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gtf","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1091,"issue_id":"bd-gtf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gtf.1","title":"L1: Hardened Interpose (default hardened)","description":"L1 Hardened Interpose: Default hardened mode for production.

Goal: Deploy FrankenLibC with hardened mode as default for improved security.

Prerequisites:
- All L0 functionality stable
- Hardened mode overhead acceptable for target workloads
- Healing policies tuned and tested

L1 Changes:
1. Default FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened (unless explicit strict)
2. Healing metrics exported to standard location
3. Documentation of healing behaviors
4. Opt-out mechanism for performance-critical paths

Validation Workloads:
- bash interactive session (terminal, job control)
- coreutils (ls, cat, grep, etc.)
- python3 -c "print('hello')"
- Simple HTTP server (Python http.server)

Performance Budget:
- <5% slowdown for typical workloads
- <200ns per libc call (hardened budget)

Testing:
- All L0 tests pass in hardened mode
- Performance regression suite
- Healing action rate monitoring
- E2E with real applications

Success Criteria:
- Hardened mode is default
- No functional regressions vs L0
- Healing improves robustness measurably

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-gtf.2","title":"L2: Partial Replace (standalone static)","description":"L2 Partial Replace: Standalone for simple static programs.

Goal: Run statically-linked simple programs without host glibc.

Prerequisites:
- CRT bootstrap complete (bd-qwm)
- Raw syscall layer complete (bd-cj0)
- No pthread call-through (bd-h5x)
- Basic stdio implemented
- C/POSIX locale complete

L2 Scope:
Programs that should work at L2:
- hello.c (minimal printf)
- Simple file I/O programs
- Pure computation programs
- Basic network clients (no resolver)

Programs explicitly out-of-scope for L2:
- Dynamically linked programs
- Programs using dlopen
- Programs needing NSS (resolver, passwd)
- Programs with complex locale needs

Technical Requirements:
1. _start entry point
2. __libc_start_main implementation
3. TLS initialization
4. atexit/exit handlers
5. Basic environ handling

Build Configuration:
- cargo build --features=standalone
- Produces libfrankenlibc_replace.a for static linking

Testing:
- Static link test suite
- Minimal hello world
- File I/O test
- Exit code propagation

Success Criteria:
- Simple static programs run without host libc
- Clean shutdown with correct exit codes
- No segfaults during CRT init/fini

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
